pages.raw_text:
The committe Of the whole, the
organization name by which the
city councilmen chose to be known
when "judiciously" bordering any
question, still persists in leaving
buried the question of who pays
for the rebuilt span on Howard
street, the city or the Washington
Water Power company that knock
ed it down.


Councilmen give as excuse an
overwhelming lot of other business
which must first be transacted.
This statement is peculiar to say
the least. Others matters of less
Importance and minor interest they
handle in hurry. They have had
buried the bridge question for
months.


Councilman Fred Baldwin, asked
whe nthe council would dig the
matter up, replied: 81 don't know
when we will reach that. We have
a stack of stuff that long first"


When he said that long" Mr..
Baldwin extended his arms like he
was trying to hug an overgrown
lady.


The fact of the matter Is the
councilmen can take the Howard
street bridge question up at the
next meeting if they so choose.


Inquiry at the city hall shows
the city is footing the bills for the
work as they are presented.


The initiative and referendum or
dinance introduced weeks ago by
Councilman Pratt is another meas
ure that has slumped into the pit.
Notwithstanding the fact that the
democratic party all over the state
has Endorsed the referendum and
made It an issue in this campaign
the democratic administration Of
Spokane stands afar off and per-
sumably thanks the Lord that it
is not as other men.
